reasoning: The stock surged ~8.4% in less than a week up to April 25 following a strong Q1 earnings beat and raised guidance on April 22. While this provides solid fundamentals and favorable analyst upgrades, such a fast spike against a broader multi-month downtrend (from 938 in Dec to 723 in early April) usually faces short-term profit-taking and technical resistance. The statistical momentum model provided a baseline probability of ~0.39 for exceeding the 819 threshold in the first week. We adjust this up to 0.42 to account for post-earnings positive drift (PEAD). An anticipated ex-dividend cutoff in late May (historically ~$4.26) may exert slight downward pressure. In strict adherence to the instruction that uncertainty should increase with horizon and probabilities for distant dates must converge towards 0.5, the estimates steadily regress toward 50/50 for the longer timeframes.
